They then saw echo fail and she hulk and said never mind and said this show was cannon to the Netflix show and completely rebooted it changing the tone a lot and adding a whole bunch of stitch scenes and lines here and there to make it cannon to the Netflix show, also changing the director the same of the punisher show.

And that right there is what made me stop watching the show after the first episode. I love Charlie cox as daredevil and Vincent as Kingpin(especially since us honest fans been saying to bring them back since the Netflix cancellation), but as soon as I saw the allegory to Trump, the blue hats, and especially the black mayor’s suit that look super close to how trump dresses in real life I just said screw this show. I didn’t vote for Trump nor do I care if anyone did, you have a right to vote for whoever you want without being labeled a “nazi”. 

But I say all this to say this crap ruins the show for me because already Disney/marvel came back with their nonsense agendas and are already telling us it’s not about telling a good comic book story it’s about pushing a message. If  Kingpin is becoming mayor of NY he would have his iconic white suit on and continue to be the imposing Force he is, but nope we are rewriting the source material for Kingpin and turning him into Donald Trump just because this side of the media don’t like him. And if that’s the case leave the kingpin alone then!

As always it’s R.I.P. to Stan Lee and all the creators and writers who actually care about Marvel comics and escapism. They would never swerve away from the source material just so they can interject their own social/political agenda into the comics.

replies.author: @robonator2945
replies.text:
"constantly" is BS. It happened a handful of times sure but it was nowhere near show-defining, and eitherway what "one rule"? We know from the first few episodes of the netflix series that he can absolutely tell if someone is alive or dead after being thrown from a building, so he should've known Bullseye was still alive, and even if he didn't I'm pretty bloody confident he's already broken that rule before. Most of his "confessionals" were just there for a convenient diagetic way for him to monologue to someone else about being daredevil.

Daredevil is a religious character, his character is not defined by his religion. I'm really getting tired of people acting like everything is soooo religious when no, it just isn't. We haven't lived in an era where people's day to day life is defined by their religion in decades if not centuries. 

Daredevil's religious aspect is a part of his character to contrast against his own darker tendancies. He naturally wants to behave one way, but his religion is both a way of facing that and avoiding it. It's somewhat akin to Batman's "no killing" rule basically. Without his religion he'd still be Daredevil though, because Daredevil *_is_* him giving into those 'darker' urges. His religion basically only exists to catalyze internal conflict, and isn't actually that necessary for his character.

replies.author: @robonator2945
replies.text:
​@tuananhnguyen1658 wowie, so cool! One minor thing though, I appear to have missed the part where I said literally fucking anything related to any of that. 

I said people were massively overplaying the religious overtones by acting as if they were inherently integral to the entire show. This is because that is objectively not true. Religion is shown only so far as it's relevant to Matt's character. You do not need to be in a church to be questioning the morality of killing someone. The religious iconography was *_there_* in the original run, and was narratively important, but you absolutely could keep all of the exact same story beats  by just changing the context a bit, because the religion isn't the important bit, it's Matt's philosophy that is. The religious overtones are just there to enable exploring his philosophy.

The religious aspects only exist insofar as they inform Matt's moral character, but if we're being honest, yeah, they're not really explored by the show itself. As far as I recall noy only do we see no other characters meaningfully engage in *_any_* religion, but I can't even recall an instance of any of the other characters talking to Matt about his religion. (to be clear, these claims are excluding the characters who are *_explicitly_* part of that aspect of Matt's life. Yeah, obviously the priests and nuns are going to talk about religion to Matt, that's not really surprising, but my point is that you don't see something like Foggy and Matt having religious arguments or something. The religion of the show only exists with respect to Matt and exploring his character) This is because that's not the point. Daredevil is not a religious show, the religion & religious aspects only exist to provide insight into Matt as a character. The confessional scenes are there as basically a stand-in for monologuing in more tropey superhero shows/movies. It's an excuse for Matt to say *_exactly_* what he is thinking or feeling so that the audience can understand his thought process diagetically.

It's already a mess and people are already jumping the ship to defend it.

author: @McClane4Ever.
text:
The show is not nearly as bad as some of you are saying. I get you are pissed at the mouse about how things have been going. This is a small step in the right direction. just watch it through "alternative means" and try to watch it with an open mind. 
And actually finish it be for you cast judgment. You won't find a bigger Netflix Daredevil fan than me and I'll say this isn't nearly as good(no one expected it to be) but it's far better than I was expecting.

replies.author: @josephkerrigan733
replies.text: What was your favourite part? Cuckpin telling Vanessa how incredible she is & how she's even more incredible now that she's taken over the criminal operations or Matt's girlfriend telling him that she saved herself from the serial killer & that Daredevil was only there "for himself" not to save her OR as a bonus the episode where Matt agreed with a career criminal who expressed no remorse or regret for constantly breaking the law & told Matt that he was to blame for him getting sent to jail when he expressly stated he would break the law whenever & wherever he wanted because he was discriminated against. Truly a vile show.

replies.author: @McClane4Ever.
replies.text:
@josephkerrigan733  
Kingpin has always been a massive simp for Vanessa. She's one of the only things he cares about(He's a spectacularly broken man not just someone-dimensional uber crime boss). Her becoming more like him and embracing more of his world would be a good thing in his eyes. He also deals with  Vanessa's betrayal in a very 'Kingpin' way.

Matt's girlfriend just went through ridiculous trauma and is trying to cope. That one is pretty obvious and shouldn't need to be explained.
Plus, the writers don't want you to like her.

When he got the guy the reduced sentence Matt was literally being a good lawyer. Doing the best he could for his client. Sure the guy was a criminal, and he got a punishment. A reduced punishment of course but that is what a lawyer does.

I like the kingpins journey in this. He started out being filmed as if he was small, a lot of high angles, other people towered over him in conversations. As his confidence returnes and he starts to embrace his old habits he starts getting bigger, physically and by the angles they use to shoot him. But my favorite part is definitely the scene in the last episode when Matt and Frank banter and then they have their showdown with the Punisher cops.

replies.author: @josephkerrigan733
replies.text:
@McClane4Ever. Good to know there's still people out there like you that will slurp up marvel slop.

Maybe in S2 Kingpin can get cucked for an entire episode since you enjoyed it so much. I also love that you think Matt getting constantly told he's incompetent & useless the entire series as he meekly agrees that he's awful is what makes a good daredevil show. Truly a awful, vile show. I'm glad it's gotten lower viewership than The Acolyte & Agatha all along. I'm sure after S2 it'll get cancelled like it deserves to.

replies.author: @McClane4Ever.
replies.text:
It's cool if you don't like it. Doesn't hurt my feelings any but I get the feeling you didn't finish the season. 

I'm not a Disney/Marvel fanboy in the least. I haven't watched anything besides Deadpool since Endgame I'm pretty sure. 

Is it as good as the old series? Nope, not even close. Is it flawed? Yes. Is it trash? far from it.

I'm glad you can enjoy such an awful show but it truly was a complete annihilation of the investment I had in Daredevil as a series & the characters it had. It was worse than I thought it would be & I'm glad it's viewership is so low that it's 100% getting cancelled after S2 is done because nobody enjoyed watching the characters that they loved get immasculated, assassinated & cucked besides some freaks.
